name: away_setpoint_change_not_override
description: Setback re-application while away must not be misdetected as a manual override
notes: Before Issue #221 fix, when the coordinator applied setback_cool (79F) on a classification
cycle while away, the thermostat_changed listener would see the setpoint change and flag it
as a manual override (manual_override_active=True). This would suppress all future setback
applications until the user returned home.
The root cause: coordinator's _async_thermostat_changed lacked an 'expected-state' guard
to distinguish CA-issued setpoint changes from user-initiated ones.
This scenario is marked track='integration' because the simulator does not model the
coordinator's _async_thermostat_changed listener â€” it requires production validation.
The simulator-level assertion confirms setback IS applied (pre-condition); the integration
assertion documents the production behavior that must be validated separately.

events.note: System is in away mode from the start of this scenario
Hot day classification â€” cool mode; away setback (79F) should be applied and must NOT trigger override detection
Steady state check: system in away mode 30 min after setback was applied

assertions.reason: Pre-condition: classification while away applies setback_cool (79F) â€” this is the setpoint change that must NOT be detected as an override in production

Integration assertion â€” requires production validation. The coordinator's _async_thermostat_changed must NOT set manual_override_active=True when CA itself issued the setback setpoint change. Simulator cannot model _async_thermostat_changed (no listener layer). Validate in production by confirming override flag remains False after a classification-cycle setback while away.

verdict.summary: CA-issued setback setpoint change while away is not flagged as a manual override
verdict.observed_behavior: Simulator confirms setback_applied (79F) passes. Production validation required for override_not_detected â€” this is a coordinator listener-layer behavior outside simulator scope.
verdict.expected_behavior: setback_applied (79F) at classification event; manual_override_active remains False in production after CA-issued setpoint change
